quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 6 Quiz 3
quiz.subtitle: Conditions That Guarantee a Quadrilateral Is a Parallelogram
quiz.subject: Geometry
quiz.topic: Geometry: 6.3 Proving a Quadrilateral Is a Parallelogram

questions.question: Which of the following is sufficient to prove a quadrilateral is a parallelogram?
options: Both pairs of opposite sides are parallel
One pair of opposite sides is parallel
questions.explanation: The definition of a parallelogram is a quadrilateral with both pairs of opposite sides parallel. Proving this alone is sufficient.

questions.question: Which condition guarantees a quadrilateral is a parallelogram?
options: Both pairs of opposite sides are congruent
One pair of opposite sides is congruent
All four angles are right angles
questions.explanation: If both pairs of opposite sides of a quadrilateral are congruent, then the quadrilateral is a parallelogram. This is one of the five main conditions.

questions.question: A quadrilateral has one pair of opposite sides that are both parallel and congruent. Is this sufficient to prove it is a parallelogram?
options: Yes, by the One Pair of Opposite Sides Theorem
No, both pairs must be parallel and congruent
No, the diagonals must also bisect each other
Yes, but only if the quadrilateral is convex
questions.explanation: If one pair of opposite sides of a quadrilateral is both parallel and congruent, then the quadrilateral is a parallelogram. This is a sufficient condition.

questions.question: A quadrilateral has diagonals that bisect each other. What does this prove?
questions.explanation: If the diagonals of a quadrilateral bisect each other, the quadrilateral is a parallelogram. Additional diagonal properties are needed for special parallelograms.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a sufficient condition to prove a quadrilateral is a parallelogram?
options: Both pairs of opposite angles are congruent
Both pairs of opposite sides are parallel
questions.explanation: Congruent diagonals alone do not guarantee a parallelogram. An isosceles trapezoid can also have congruent diagonals. The other three conditions are sufficient to prove a quadrilateral is a parallelogram.

questions.question: In quadrilateral $ABCD$, $\angle A \cong \angle C$ and $\angle B \cong \angle D$. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If both pairs of opposite angles of a quadrilateral are congruent, then the quadrilateral is a parallelogram.

questions.question: In quadrilateral $PQRS$, $\overline{PQ} \cong \overline{RS}$ and $\overline{PS} \cong \overline{QR}$. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If both pairs of opposite sides are congruent, the quadrilateral is a parallelogram. This is a direct application of one of the five conditions.

questions.question: In quadrilateral $WXYZ$, $\overline{WX} \parallel \overline{ZY}$ and $\overline{WX} \cong \overline{ZY}$. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If one pair of opposite sides is both parallel and congruent, the quadrilateral is a parallelogram.

questions.question: In quadrilateral $ABCD$, diagonals $\overline{AC}$ and $\overline{BD}$ intersect at $E$. If $AE \cong EC$ and $BE \cong ED$, what must be true?
questions.explanation: If the diagonals bisect each other (each diagonal's midpoint is the intersection point), the quadrilateral is a parallelogram.

questions.question: How many distinct conditions are there to prove a quadrilateral is a parallelogram?
questions.explanation: There are five conditions: (1) both pairs of opposite sides parallel, (2) both pairs of opposite sides congruent, (3) both pairs of opposite angles congruent, (4) diagonals bisect each other, (5) one pair of opposite sides both parallel and congruent.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered all five conditions for proving a quadrilateral is a parallelogram and can distinguish sufficient conditions from insufficient ones.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the main conditions but review why congruent diagonals alone do not guarantee a parallelogram.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: five ways to prove a parallelogram — opposite sides parallel, opposite sides congruent, opposite angles congruent, diagonals bisect each other, one pair of opposite sides both parallel and congruent.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! The five conditions are: (1) both pairs of opposite sides ∥, (2) both pairs of opposite sides ≅, (3) both pairs of opposite angles ≅, (4) diagonals bisect each other, (5) one pair of opposite sides both ∥ and ≅.
